prompt:
[Reading · Policy]

> **Employee Handbook — Paid Time Off (excerpt)**
> Full-time employees accrue 1.25 PTO days per month starting on their hire date. Accrual pauses during unpaid leave longer than 14 calendar days. PTO requests require manager approval at least 5 business days before the first day off, except for emergency sick leave.

An employee hired March 1 takes 10 unpaid days starting June 1. When does PTO accrual resume?

[Reading · Email]

> **From:** ops-leads@company.com
> **Subject:** Q3 maintenance window
> **Body:** We will migrate the billing database Saturday 02:00–06:00 UTC. Checkout will be read-only; invoices already queued will still send. Rollback plan is documented in RUN-4821. Reply-all only for blockers.

Which customer-facing action remains available during the window?
options: Sending invoices already in the queue

prompt:
[Reading · Meeting notes]

> **Design review — Checkout refresh (2026-07-08)**
> - PM: ship guest checkout in phase 1; account linking deferred.
> - Legal: cookie banner must block analytics until consent.
> - Eng: estimate 3 sprints if fraud rules stay unchanged.
> - **Action:** PM to circulate revised wireframes by Friday.

In one sentence, what is the PM's assigned follow-up and its deadline?
rubric: Must state PM will circulate/send revised wireframes and that the deadline is Friday (2026-07-11 acceptable). No need to mention other topics.

prompt:
[Reading · FAQ]

> **Internal IT FAQ — VPN**
> Q: Can I use split tunneling?
> A: No. All traffic must route through corporate VPN on managed laptops.
> Q: What if my home ISP blocks UDP 443?
> A: Switch to TCP profile "corp-tcp" in the VPN client settings.

A developer's home network blocks UDP 443. What should they do first?
options: Request a static IP from IT

prompt:
[Reading · Memo]

> **Facilities memo — Denver office**
> Badge access to floors 3–5 is unchanged. Floor 6 lab moves to Building B on Aug 1; until then, escorted access only via reception. Parking validation for visitors ends July 31.

Before August 1, how can an employee enter the floor 6 lab?

[Reading · Spec snippet]

```
Feature: Invoice PDF export
- Must include remittance address block
- Line items sorted by service date ascending
- Tax shown only when tax_code present
- Filename: INV-{invoice_id}.pdf
```

Which requirement applies when tax_code is absent?
options: Use a default tax rate of 8.25%

prompt:
[Reading · Contract clause]

> **MSA Section 4.2 — Data retention**
> Customer data is deleted within 30 days of contract termination unless a litigation hold notice is active. Backups rotate every 7 days and are purged after 35 days. Holds supersede automatic deletion.

A customer terminates on July 1 with no litigation hold. By what date must primary customer data be deleted?
rubric: Must compute 30 days after July 1 → July 31 (or 'within 30 days of termination'). Should not cite backup rotation unless noting primary data deadline.

prompt:
[Reading · Release notes]

> **App v4.12.0**
> - Fixed: push notifications duplicated on Android 14.
> - Changed: session timeout now 30 minutes of inactivity (was 60).
> - Known issue: CSV export truncates notes over 2,000 characters — fix planned v4.12.1.

A user reports missing text in exported CSV notes. What is the best immediate guidance?
options: Clear app cache and retry export
Wait for v4.12.1 or shorten notes before export
Reinstall the app to fix notifications

prompt:
[Reading · SLA excerpt]

> **Support SLA — Business tier**
> P1: 15-minute first response, updates every hour until mitigated.
> P2: 1-hour first response, updates every 4 hours.
> P3: Next business day first response.
> Maintenance windows excluded from SLA clocks.

A P2 ticket is opened at 09:00 on a business day. By when must the first agent response occur?

[Reading · Handbook]

> **Expense policy — Meals**
> Individual meals while traveling: up to $45 USD per day in the US, $55 in Canada. Alcohol is not reimbursable. Receipts required over $25. Team dinners require pre-approval when expected to exceed $75 per person.

A traveler in Austin submits a $38 lunch receipt with no alcohol. Is pre-approval required?
options: No, it is within the daily cap and under $75 team threshold
No, but alcohol must be itemized

prompt:
[Reading · Policy table]

| Role | Can approve PO | Max amount (USD) |
|------|----------------|------------------|
| Manager | Yes | 5,000 |
| Director | Yes | 25,000 |
| VP | Yes | 100,000 |
| Individual contributor | No | — |

A manager submits a $6,200 PO for approval. Who must approve it per the table?
rubric: Must identify Director or higher (manager cap is $5,000). Credit for citing the $5,000 manager limit.

prompt:
[Reading · Email thread]

> **From:** vendor@acmesupply.com
> **Subject:** Partial shipment #8841
> **Body:** Line 3 (SKU-992) backordered 3 weeks. Lines 1–2 shipped today via FedEx 7844 1234 5678. Remaining items will ship no partial credits until full order ships unless you cancel line 3.

Which items shipped today?

[Reading · Spec]

```
API: POST /v1/refunds
- Idempotency-Key header required
- Partial refunds allowed if amount <= captured_amount
- Refunds to original payment method only
- 409 if charge still pending capture
```

A support agent retries the same refund request with the same Idempotency-Key after a timeout. Expected behavior?

[Reading · Internal memo]

> **HR memo — Performance cycle**
> Self-reviews open Aug 1; manager reviews due Aug 21. Calibration is Aug 25–27. Employees on leave >30 days by Aug 1 use simplified review template unless they request standard.

When are manager-written reviews due?

[Reading · Policy exceptions]

> **Remote work policy**
> Core hours 10:00–15:00 local time. Two office days/month required for roles tagged "onsite-flex." Exceptions for medical accommodation documented with HR only — not manager discretion.

An employee asks their manager to waive office days due to childcare. What does policy require?
rubric: Must state exceptions go through HR / documented accommodation — not informal manager waiver. Credit for citing core hours only if tied to wrong path.

prompt:
[Reading · RFP excerpt]

> **RFP scoring — Section 2**
> Price 30%, Implementation timeline 25%, References 20%, Security questionnaire 25%. Vendors missing SOC 2 Type II at submission are disqualified regardless of score.

A vendor scores highest on price and timeline but lacks SOC 2 Type II at submission. Outcome?

[Reading · Meeting notes]

> **Incident review — API latency**
> Root cause: connection pool mis-sized after scale-up. Mitigation: pool max raised 50→200. Follow-up: add autoscaling alert on pool wait time >100ms. **Not in scope:** client retry logic change.

Which follow-up item was explicitly marked out of scope?
options: Autoscaling alert on pool wait time

prompt:
[Reading · Vendor email]

> **From:** billing@cloudhost.io
> Your reserved instance discount applies only to us-east-1 Linux instances tagged `env=prod`. Discount does not apply to Windows or spot instances. True-up invoices post quarterly.

Which instance would NOT receive the reserved discount?
options: Linux spot in us-east-1 tagged env=prod

prompt:
[Reading · Compliance bulletin]

> **Privacy bulletin — Q2**
> New DSAR workflow: legal triage within 2 business days; fulfillment within 30 calendar days. Marketing opt-out requests still route to marketing@, not legal.

Summarize who handles a marketing unsubscribe request vs. a formal DSAR.
rubric: Must distinguish marketing opt-out → marketing@ (or marketing team) vs DSAR → legal workflow with triage/30-day fulfillment. Both paths required.

prompt:
[Reading · Product brief]

> **Brief — Vendor portal MVP**
> Must-have: invoice upload, status tracking. Nice-to-have: chat. Out of scope: payment processing (phase 2). Launch target: Oct 15 if security review completes by Sep 1.

Is payment processing included in the MVP scope?

[Reading · Policy cross-reference]

> **Travel — Booking**
> Fly economy under 6 hours; business class requires VP pre-approval. **Car rental:** compact class default; upgrades with safety justification only.
>
> **Travel — Reimbursement**
> Personal loyalty points cannot be reimbursed; only out-of-pocket amounts.

An employee books business class for a 4-hour flight using personal points plus $200 copay. Which policy applies to the copay reimbursement?
options: Economy cap applies; copay not reimbursable
Business class forbidden; copay not reimbursable
VP approval needed; only $200 reimbursable if approved
Full fare reimbursable because points used

prompt:
[Reading · Legal summary]

> **NDA summary (non-lawyer)**
> 3-year term; mutual; covers business info marked confidential or reasonably understood confidential. Excludes public info and independently developed work. Injunction remedy available for breach.

Engineer rebuilds a feature from public documentation without using confidential docs. NDA breach?
options: No, public info and independent development are excluded

prompt:
[Reading · Multi-doc scenario]

> **Email:** "Please enable feature flag `beta_reports` for Acme only."
>
> **Flag registry:**
> `beta_reports` — default off; allowed tenants: acme, beta-corp; requires analytics consent flag `analytics_on`.
>
> **Acme tenant record:** analytics_on = false

List the steps (in order) to enable `beta_reports` for Acme.
correct_answer_exact: Enable analytics_on, then enable beta_reports for Acme.
rubric: Must enable/set analytics_on first, then enable beta_reports for Acme tenant. Order matters; credit for noting both flags.

prompt:
[Reading · Audit finding]

> **Internal audit — Access reviews**
> Finding: 12 contractors retained VPN access >30 days after end date. Policy requires removal within 24 hours. Root cause: offboarding ticket not linked to IAM workflow.

What process fix best addresses the root cause?
options: Link offboarding tickets to IAM deprovisioning

prompt:
[Reading · Escalation policy]

> **Escalation matrix**
> Billing disputes >$10k → Finance director within 4 business hours.
> Security incidents with data exposure → CISO immediately, then legal within 1 hour.
> PR inquiries → Comms only; do not comment on active incidents.

Media asks about a suspected data exposure. Who should respond first per policy?

[Reading · Cross-functional brief]

> **Launch brief excerpt**
> Marketing may announce GA on Sep 10 only if: (1) SOC 2 letter received, (2) support runbooks published, (3) rollback tested in staging. Engineering sign-off alone is insufficient.

Engineering certifies rollback tested, but runbooks are draft. Can marketing announce GA on Sep 10?

[Reading · Change log]

> **Pricing update memo**
> Effective Sep 1: Pro plan $49→$59/month; Grandfathering until Dec 31 for contracts signed before Aug 15. Enterprise custom — unaffected.

A customer signed Pro on Aug 10. When does the new price apply?
rubric: Must use grandfathering rule: price stays $49 until Dec 31, then $59 (or new price after grandfather ends). Aug 10 is before Aug 15 cutoff.

prompt:
[Reading · Regulatory notice]

> **State notice — Sales tax nexus**
> Remote sellers must collect tax in State X once sales exceed $100,000 or 200 transactions in the prior calendar year. Registration required within 30 days of threshold crossing.

Company hit $105,000 in State X sales on Nov 15. Registration deadline?

[Reading · Board summary]

> **Board risk summary**
> Top risk: vendor concentration (42% spend on Vendor A). Mitigation: dual-source critical components by Q2 next year. **Do not disclose** un-audited churn figures externally.

Which topic is flagged as not for external disclosure?

[Reading · Policy synthesis]

> **Data classification**
> Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted.
>
> **Email DLP rule:** Restricted data may not leave the org via email. Confidential may leave only to approved domains list.
>
> **Approved domains:** `@partnerco.com`, `@auditfirm.com`

An employee emails a Confidential spreadsheet to `@clientstartup.io`. Allowed?
options: No, domain not on approved list
